Name: AOD as the Engine
Short Description: At the centre is the Agency Opportunity Dashboard.
Rich Text:
Each participating agency has its own AOD instance. It acts as an engine that continually surfaces where fundamentals are holding up and where they are slipping across the agency’s chosen client sites. This supports retention conversations, clearer mid-engagement reviews, and gives agencies stronger evidence in new business discussions.

Signals from AiSC feed into the AOD so account teams can see, in one place, where client conversations are most justified. Which sites are losing ground. Which clients appear more exposed. Where there is a clear basis for additional support, advisory work, or targeted projects.

Instead of relying on scattered updates from individual accounts, agencies can use the AOD in regular internal reviews as a structured view of opportunity and risk across their portfolio, and as a prompt for timely, relevant contact with clients.

Name: Advocacy
Short Description: Consistent results make it easy for clients to explain the relationship and recommend the agency.
Rich Text:
Advocacy develops when clients can describe the relationship using clear, repeatable evidence. Over time, the summaries build a record that shows how issues have been surfaced, prioritised, and addressed. This record is easy to share with colleagues and leadership. It provides a straightforward explanation of the value the agency delivers.

Because the information is simple to interpret, clients do not need to rely on subjective statements. They can point to specific reports that illustrate progress and stability. This makes it easier to justify ongoing spend and to introduce the agency to other teams or departments.

Advocacy also appears in how often the agency is invited into new discussions. When fundamentals are clearly monitored and understood, the agency becomes a natural partner for future plans. Their reputation is based on consistent control and steady progress, not occasional success or unpredictable cycles of issue resolution.

Clients who feel informed and supported become natural promoters of the relationship, helping the agency gain visibility inside and outside the organisation.

‍

“Consistent results make the agency easy to explain and recommend.”

Using; ASSURANCE

Name: Awareness
Short Description: Evidence helps agencies start conversations with substance and stand out from generic competitors.
Rich Text:
Awareness is when a prospect first notices the agency. Instead of relying on broad claims or creative showcases, the agency introduces evidence drawn from measured fundamentals. Using high level results created through the platform, agencies highlight where sectors or specific organisations may be underperforming. This changes the starting point. Prospects see something concrete, not a general opinion about digital practice.

Agencies can use individual site summaries for direct outreach, or aggregate sector results for broader campaigns and PR. These outputs highlight where value is slipping or where unnecessary risk is increasing. Because the information is structured and repeatable, it supports email contact, webinars, content marketing, and targeted approaches without adding complexity.

Awareness becomes stronger because the first interaction carries relevance. A prospect can see that the agency understands the landscape and can speak about fundamentals using measured results. That creates a natural reason for a conversation. The agency is positioned as practical, informed, and focused on improving the prospect’s situation rather than making generic introductory claims.

‍

“Evidence creates interest and helps agencies cut through noise.”

Using; INSIGHT

Name: Brand Promise
Short Description: ‍We create better digital experiences for users and give content owners the confidence that their content is truly ready.
Rich Text:
Our tools support the entire content lifecycle - helping teams manage digital content quality from visibility to action.
Users benefit from content that is accessible, usable, and purposeful. Owners gain clarity, control, and assurance that what they publish is ready to deliver value and is in full compliance.

Name: Buyer perception
Short Description: Rose tinted glasses hinder clarity.
Rich Text:
Have we now reached a point where many agencies appear similar? Pitches, websites, and case studies often blur together, making it harder for clients to see meaningful differences.

At the same time, post-launch visibility has become a weak point. Clients do not always know how their website is performing or where unnecessary risk is emerging, so they bring in tools and advisors to answer those questions, which shifts attention and budget away from the agency.

We believe this creates both a challenge and an opportunity. The challenge is increased noise and competitive pressure. The opportunity is providing clients with evidence that is structured, consistent, and commercially relevant.

Agencies that can do this gain a more stable position in accounts and a clearer advantage when competing for new work.

Name: Engagement
Short Description: Clear results give agencies a shared reference point to discuss a prospect’s current situation.
Rich Text:
Engagement is when interest becomes a detailed conversation. The agency presents a factual view of where the prospect stands today, based on summaries of Value and Risk across the fundamentals. This creates a shared foundation for discussion. The agency is not relying on assumptions. It is showing the prospect a structured way to understand their current position.

Depending on the opportunity, this may involve a single site or a small group of sites. For larger proposals, the agency can explain how this view will continue each month once work begins. The prospect sees both the starting point and the practical method the agency uses to maintain visibility over time.

This approach reduces uncertainty. Senior stakeholders do not have to interpret technical reports. They see a consistent framework that highlights areas performing well and areas that need attention. Engagement becomes focused and grounded in evidence rather than subjective assessments. The agency demonstrates how it will create clarity from day one and maintain that clarity across the relationship.

‍

“Clear results give both sides a shared starting point for discussion.”

Using; INSIGHT / ASSURANCE

Name: Future value
Short Description: Who builds strength as Ai reshapes markets.
Rich Text:
As Ai accelerates, the winners will be businesses that bring structure and clarity to complex environments. Advantage will come from knowing where things stand, protecting what matters, and using automation without creating unnecessary risk.

Many tools promise speed. Few build confidence. The strongest models will be technical, durable, and unglamorous. They sit inside critical operations, deliver repeatable outcomes, and retain control of their intellectual property.

AiSC fits this pattern. It lives where technical depth meets ongoing operational need. It removes friction, sharpens visibility, and protects value over time.

‍

‍

Name: Growth
Short Description: Visible patterns highlight practical opportunities to expand work and deepen existing account value.
Rich Text:
Growth emerges when patterns in the summaries reveal areas where focused work will make a measurable difference. Repeated issues, stalled improvements, or gaps between sites provide a clear basis for new proposals. The agency is not making speculative suggestions. It is referring to information the client already sees each month.

This reduces the effort required to justify new work. Clients understand the relevance because they have already discussed the underlying issues. The conversation becomes about scope, sequencing, and priorities, not about convincing the client that a problem exists.

Growth also includes revenue that now remains inside the agency. Oversight and related content work are less likely to drift to external suppliers or unmanaged internal effort. As more sites or teams adopt the model, the structure scales without significant additional burden.

The result is a broader, more stable relationship. The agency gains deeper involvement across the digital estate and provides support that aligns directly with measured needs. Growth becomes a natural extension of the consistency already in place, not a separate sales activity.

‍

“Patterns highlight practical opportunities to expand work already underway.”

Using; INSIGHT / REMEDIATION

Name: Leaving Money on the Table
Short Description: Client retention, growing revenue.
Rich Text:
Agencies operate in a market where it is difficult to stand out. Competitors sound similar, creating noise that makes lead generation and pitch differentiation harder. Even strong agencies can struggle to show how they add value in a way clients immediately understand.

After a site goes live, another challenge appears. Clients still need clarity on what is happening across their website, yet agencies are not always asked to provide this in a structured, ongoing way. Without that clarity, clients often turn to third party tools or external advisors to understand where things stand. These tools highlight issues in isolation, create conflicting narratives, and shift attention away from the agency.

This combination leaves money on the table. Clients divert budget into tools and suppliers that sit between them and the agency. Opportunities for support or improvement are missed or delayed. Instead of forward-looking conversations, time is spent managing opinions, dealing with confusion, and trying to re-establish control.

In a crowded market, the agency loses both revenue and a clear point of difference at the moment where clarity should strengthen the relationship.

Name: Our Proposition
Short Description: ‍Our global infrastructure supports thousands of users across multiple regions, delivering compliance automation at scale with built-in resilience and failover.
Rich Text:
The platform is designed for ease of use, requiring minimal training or support and is backed by a 24/7 partner network that ensures continuous coverage. Whether you’re a global enterprise or a local team, AAAnow is ready to scale with you.

‍

Three products. One joined-up approach.

AAAnow offers a suite of tools that work together to support the management of digital compliance - from visibility to action.
‍‍

AiSC: The technology for digital content rating

AiSC independently benchmarks digital content against six digital fundamentals: Accessibility, Privacy, Experience, Search, Integrity, and Compliance.
It replaces guesswork with objective clarity, helping leaders see where they stand, focus effort where it counts, and track progress at scale.‍

‍
‍AiSM: The technology for digital content diagnostics

AiSM provides deep diagnostic insight, highlighting root causes of issues across SEO, cookies, and other critical compliance and performance areas.
It’s a precision tool that helps web teams act faster, reduce exposure, and resolve problems before they escalate.


AiCM: The technology for digital content resolution

AiCM automatically finds and fixes broken, outdated, or non-compliant content. It also enables users to tailor their individual experience through accessible front-end overlays.
It delivers content that’s accurate, inclusive by design, and easier to manage – quickly and at scale.


Each tool solves a different part of the problem - but they work best when combined. Together, they close the loop - providing clarity, priority, and measurable progress across your digital estate. This end-to-end capability moves from diagnosis to resolution.

Name: Our Value
Short Description: We help teams measure, compare, and improve the quality of digital content.
Rich Text:
‍Our value lies in providing the tools, insight, and automation needed to effectively manage compliance complexity with AI-driven automation.
‍
This helps teams reduce risks, lower costs, drive results, and enable Lead Generation, while freeing them to focus on priorities. Our approach delivers measurable progress at speed and scale, offers independent, objective insights, provides the data-driven confidence needed to act and report responsibly, and ultimately ensures content owners have the assurance that their content is truly ready, benefiting users with better digital experiences.

Executives gain confidence in their digital estate through clarity and independent verification, allowing them to make informed decisions with full oversight.

Name: Purchase
Short Description: Structured oversight removes client distraction and brings order and clarity to ongoing support work.
Rich Text:
Purchase is the point where the working model is agreed. The agency positions structured oversight as part of its support approach, helping the client reduce the effort and distraction associated with monitoring content and fundamentals. Monthly summaries highlight where Value is improving, where Risk is rising, and where action is needed. These summaries are predictable, consistent, and easy to interpret.

Where deeper content work is required, the agency can provide a structured view of the actions involved. The client chooses whether to allocate this work internally or include it within the agency’s support package. Either way, the agency provides a clear and repeatable mechanism for understanding priorities.

This reduces the client’s manual checking and removes the uncertainty of ad hoc findings. The agency avoids leaving money on the table by incorporating oversight and action into the contract rather than allowing this work to drift elsewhere.

The relationship starts with clarity and sets expectations around how issues will be seen, communicated, and addressed. The client no longer carries the burden of chasing detail, and the agency maintains a more organised and predictable workflow

‍

“Structured oversight removes distraction and brings order to support work.”

Using; INSIGHT / REMEDIATION

Name: Retention
Short Description: Regular reporting keeps progress visible, reduces surprises, and stabilises long term client relationships.
Rich Text:
Retention is driven by consistent, factual reporting that shows how fundamentals are being maintained. Each monthly summary presents a clear picture of the current position, highlighting improvements made and new issues emerging. This removes the uncertainty that often causes dissatisfaction, where clients only discover problems once they become visible or disruptive.

For agencies, these summaries create a stable rhythm for communication. Instead of waiting for issues to be raised, they contact clients with an update based on measured findings. Discussions become orderly and predictable. Priorities can be agreed quickly because both sides are working from the same reference point.

Retention also benefits internal efficiency. Automated oversight reduces manual work and frees team time for planning, explanation, and higher value support. The agency becomes easier to work with because surprises reduce and communication feels regular and reliable.

Clients remain confident because they see ongoing attention to fundamentals rather than reactive fire fighting. This stability avoids unnecessary churn and ensures that value stays within the relationship instead of leaking to external tools or unmanaged internal processes.

‍

“Regular reporting keeps progress visible and reduces surprises.”

Using; ASSURANCE

Name: What Actually Matters
Short Description: What actually matters is ensuring digital content is ready to be online.
Rich Text:
‍‍What actually matters is ensuring digital content is ready to be online, which involves demonstrating accountability, managing risk (reputational, legal, financial), and maximising value.
‍
It's about having evidence-based compliance and gaining confidence in your digital estate, leading to better oversight and clearer reporting for executives.

The fundamentals

Trying to deal with everything, on every page - not efficient, and certainly not practical (why fix something on a page no one looks etc - when on a key landing page there is a missing title?)  - the key is to deal with what matters, what makes a difference; what we refer to as the fundamentals.

The fundamentals, the basis of AiSC reporting:

  • Experience
  • Accessibility
  • Carbon
  • Privacy
  • Integrity
  • Search (SEO)

Name: What this unlocks for agencies
Short Description: This combination unlocks three practical outcomes for agencies.
Rich Text:
AiSC introduces a new layer of intelligence that opens doors, improves retention, and expands accounts through evidence framed in terms clients can connect directly to outcomes.

‍

First,

it helps protect existing revenue.

When the agency provides structured, continuous evidence, clients have fewer reasons to bring in tools that position themselves as the primary source of insight and compete for service work.

This reduces the time and cost clients spend managing extra suppliers and helps the agency hold its place as the main digital partner.

‍

Second,

it creates more targeted growth.

The AOD highlights where there is money left on the table in existing accounts.

Account teams can approach clients with specific, evidence backed conversations rather than broad upsell attempts.

This supports more billable days, stronger retainers, and longer contracts, grounded in identified need and visible opportunity, and strengthens the agency’s narrative in new business..

‍

Third,

it offers a clearer point of difference.

Agencies can show they are using AI driven, disruptive innovation in a way that is efficient and commercially useful.

They bring a layer of intelligence into pitches and reviews that helps them stand out in a crowded market and supports a shift from chasing new pitches to growing existing relationships.

Name: What’s different – why AiSC?
Short Description: It’s about positive use of innovation, disruptive in a meaningful way.
Rich Text:
We focus on giving commercial teams clear evidence. Our Ai is the advantage they can use from the first conversation.

This gives CEOs, BD, and account teams something clear and factual to lead with, so they can talk about value, risk, and opportunity without waiting for a technical audit or a one off report. Technology supports the conversation rather than complicating it.

AiSC applies Ai and automation to the parts of digital operations that have been hard to monitor in a structured way. It looks at what is happening across sites over time and frames the results in terms that support client decisions, revenue protection, and account growth. This makes it easier for commercial teams to see where to engage, which clients to prioritise, and what to put on the agenda next.

This is where the disruption sits. Instead of technical teams being brought in at the end to explain problems, commercial teams can open doors with clear evidence, then bring specialists in when action is needed. The result is a more efficient, helpful way of working, where technology quietly does the heavy lifting in the background and the agency can concentrate on relationships, advice, and delivery.

Name: Why this exists
Short Description: The gap in how many agencies stay involved after launch.
Rich Text:
Agencies focus on winning projects, delivering builds, and running campaigns. Their role once a site is live is often less structured, with retainers framed around hours and tasks rather than a clear, ongoing responsibility for how the site is performing or where unnecessary risk is emerging. This makes it harder to show continuing value in reviews and new business conversations.

At the same time, client websites keep growing. Content, search behavior, accessibility expectations, privacy rules, and Ai driven discovery change faster than traditional reporting cycles. Clients still need clarity. When the agency does not provide it in a structured way, clients look elsewhere and start relying on tools or external advisors, which can dilute the agency’s position at several points in the lifecycle.

AiSC is designed to support agencies with a more consistent, commercially framed view of what is happening across client sites, so their post-launch role has clear purpose.

Name: Worldview
Short Description: Why agencies, why AiSC, the reason for a growth engine.
Rich Text: We believe agencies create the most value when they can work with evidence that supports their advice, their creativity, and their ongoing role after a site goes live. Strong work alone can be overlooked in a noisy market, and clients want straightforward understanding of how their website is supporting or undermining their goals. Account teams want structure. Leaders want to know where value is improving or slipping.We see a digital landscape that moves faster than clients can track, and where the tools they buy often create confusion rather than confidence. In this environment, agencies need steady, factual, commercially framed evidence to anchor conversations, avoid unnecessary noise, and show the part they play in protecting and strengthening value.
